tldr: Headlines across all nine country buckets report Germany's acquisition of U.S. Tomahawk cruise missiles as a bilateral security deal struck at the NATO Ankara summit; the framing is nearly uniform except for German tabloid Bild, which names Trump personally and labels the missiles 'Anti-Putin-Raketen,' and Italy's La Stampa, which leads instead with the Kremlin's separate criticism of U.S. arms supplies to Ukraine.

frames.sub_frame: Germany buys US Tomahawk cruise missiles

evidence.quote: US approves sale of Tomahawk missiles to Germany
US approves sale of Tomahawk missiles to Germany
Мерц заявил о согласии США продать Германии ракеты
Merz says Germany agreed to acquire US Tomahawk missiles

frames.sub_frame: US-NATO bilateral deal at Ankara summit

evidence.quote: German chancellor announces plan to purchase US Tomahawk
Germany agreed to buy Tomahawk missiles at NATO summit
Il Cremlino: gli Usa armano Kiev coi Patriot
Merz says Germany agreed to acquire US Tomahawk missiles
Germany, US agree at NATO summit on Berlin's purchase

single_outlet_findings.finding: Only outlet to name Trump personally ('Tomahawk-Deal mit Trump perfekt') and brand the missiles 'Anti-Putin-Raketen,' converting a bilateral defense acquisition into a political-adversarial narrative absent from every other headline in the corpus.

Only headline to depart from the Tomahawk/Germany story entirely, leading instead with the Kremlin's accusation that the U.S. arms Ukraine with Patriots while claiming to want peace — embedding the cluster's core event in the Russia-Ukraine war frame rather than NATO alliance dynamics.

bottom_line: The headline layer presents near-uniform security and bilateral-deal framing of Germany's Tomahawk purchase across nine country buckets, with German tabloid Bild the lone voice to cast the deal in Trump-centric, anti-Russian terms, and Italy's La Stampa the only outlet to redirect to the Kremlin's critique of U.S. arms to Ukraine.
